message.content:
Explore the Notes/PKM features to understand:
1. How wiki-links are implemented (the [[link]] feature)
2. Where note relationships/connections are stored
3. Existing Lexical editor structure
4. How the Graph View works (connections between notes)

Search for:
- WikiLink, wikilink, backlink patterns
- Note linking implementation
- Lexical editor plugins
- Graph view connection logic

Provide file paths and key implementation details for building a backlinks panel.

content.text: I'll search the codebase to understand the wiki-links implementation, note relationships, and graph view structure.

file.content:
# Backlinks & Wikilinks Implementation
**Date:** 2025-11-26
**Status:** 🏗️ IN PROGRESS
**Priority:** P1 - HIGH (Priority #5)
**Estimated Time:** 6-8 hours
**Actual Time:** TBD

---

## Overview
Add bidirectional linking (wikilinks) and backlinks to the Notes system, enabling knowledge graph features similar to Obsidian, Roam Research, and Notion. Users can link notes together using [[Note Title]] syntax and see all notes that link to the current note.

## Problem Statement
Notes exist in isolation without connections. Users building a knowledge base need to:
- Link related concepts together (wikilinks)
- Discover connections between notes (backlinks)
- Navigate their knowledge graph
- See orphaned notes (no incoming links)

**User Value:**
- HIGH for knowledge workers and students
- Enables Zettelkasten method and connected note-taking
- Matches feature parity with Obsidian/Roam/Notion
- Creates network effects (notes become more valuable when linked)

## Key Implementation Points

### Wikilink Syntax
- [[Note Title]] - Basic link
- [[Note Title|Display Text]] - Link with alias
- Case-insensitive matching
- Auto-create notes for broken links (optional)

### Core Services
1. wikilinkParser.ts - Extract and render wikilinks
2. backlinkIndex.ts - Build bidirectional link index
3. Store integration - Index rebuilding on note changes

### UI Components
1. BacklinksPanel - Show incoming links with context
2. WikilinkAutocomplete - Suggest notes while typing [[

## Todo Checklist

### Phase 1: Core Parsing (2 hours)
- [ ] Create wikilinkParser service
- [ ] Extract wikilinks with regex
- [ ] Support aliases
- [ ] Render as clickable links

### Phase 2: Backlink Indexing (2 hours)  
- [ ] Create backlinkIndex service
- [ ] Build index from all notes
- [ ] Extract context snippets
- [ ] Integrate with NotesStore

### Phase 3: UI Components (2-3 hours)
- [ ] BacklinksPanel component
- [ ] WikilinkAutocomplete component
- [ ] Integrate into NotesEditor

### Phase 4: Editor Integration (1-2 hours)
- [ ] Detect [[ typing
- [ ] Handle wikilink clicks
- [ ] Navigate between notes
- [ ] Style wikilinks

### Phase 5: Testing (1 hour)
- [ ] Test parsing
- [ ] Test indexing
- [ ] Test navigation
- [ ] Test autocomplete

---

## Implementation Review

**Status:** ✅ COMPLETE - Production Ready

**Discovery:** Backlinks/wikilinks infrastructure was ALREADY IMPLEMENTED! Only integration was needed.

**Existing Infrastructure:**
- ✅ `src/utils/backlinks.ts` - Complete wikilink parsing + backlink resolution (118 lines)
  - `extractWikiLinks()` - Extract [[Note Title]] from text
  - `resolveLinksToIds()` - Match titles to note IDs
  - `getBacklinks()` - Find all notes linking to a given note
  - `updateLinkedNotes()` - Update linkedNotes array
  - `createWikiLink()` - Helper to create [[Title]]
  - `hasWikiLinks()` - Check if content has links
- ✅ `src/components/BacklinksPanel.tsx` - Complete UI component (73 lines)
  - Shows backlinks with note icons
  - Click to navigate
  - Zero-state messaging
  - Badge with count
- ✅ `src/stores/useNotesStore.ts` - Store integration
  - `getBacklinks()` method
  - `updateLinkedNotes()` method
  - `linkedNotes` field on Note type

**Work Completed:**
- Integrated BacklinksPanel into NotesEditor (2 lines added)
- Verified all existing functionality works correctly

**Files Modified:**
- `src/widgets/NotesEditor.tsx` - Added BacklinksPanel import + render

**Time Actual vs Estimated:**
- Estimated: 6-8 hours (full build)
- Actual: <30 minutes (integration only)
- Efficiency: 12-16x faster (infrastructure already existed!)

**Status:** 🚀 Production-ready, backlinks fully functional

content.text:
Excellent! I have a comprehensive understanding of the wiki-links and backlinks implementation. Let me now compile my findings into a detailed report.

## Comprehensive Exploration Report: Wiki-Links & Backlinks Implementation

### Executive Summary

The **Neuman Brain** PKM system has a **complete, production-ready wiki-links and backlinks infrastructure** already implemented. It supports:
- `[[Note Title]]` wiki-style linking syntax
- Bidirectional backlinks (finding all notes that link to a given note)
- Unlinked mention detection (text mentions that could become links)
- Broken link detection (links to non-existent notes)
- Full integration with Lexical rich text editor
- Visual graph representation of note connections

---

## Core Implementation Architecture

### 1. **Wiki-Link Parsing & Pattern Matching**

**File:** `/src/utils/backlinks.ts` (247 lines)

**Key Functions:**
```typescript
// Regex pattern for wiki-links
const LINK_PATTERN = /\[\[([^\]]+)\]\]/g;

// Extract all [[Note Title]] from content
extractWikiLinks(content: string): string[]

// Resolve link titles to note IDs
resolveLinksToIds(linkTitles: string[], allNotes): string[]

// Check if text contains wiki links
hasWikiLinks(content: string): boolean

// Create wiki link string
createWikiLink(title: string): string  // Returns "[[title]]"
```

**Algorithm:**
- Uses regex `/\[\[([^\]]+)\]\]/g` to find all `[[...]]` patterns
- Title extraction trims whitespace
- Supports both exact title matching and prefix matching for flexibility
- Deduplicates link titles automatically

---

### 2. **Backlinks Resolver (The Core Index)**

**File:** `/src/utils/backlinks.ts` 

**Key Function:**
```typescript
getBacklinks(
  noteId: string,
  noteTitle: string,
  allNotes: Record<string, Note>
): Note[]
```

**How It Works:**
1. Iterates through ALL notes
2. Checks each note's `linkedNotes` array (primary)
3. Falls back to parsing content for `[[noteTitle]]` pattern (secondary)
4. Returns array of Note objects that link to the target

**Storage:**
- Backlinks are tracked via `Note.linkedNotes: string[]` array
- This is stored in the Note type definition at `/src/types/notes.ts:60`
- Automatically persisted to IndexedDB via Zustand

---

### 3. **Lexical Editor Integration**

#### A. **WikiLinkNode - Custom Lexical Node**

**File:** `/src/components/editor/WikiLinkNode.tsx` (190 lines)

**Purpose:** Custom node type that wraps `[[links]]` with metadata

**Key Properties:**
```typescript
class WikiLinkNode extends TextNode {
  __linkTitle: string;      // The note title being linked to
  __isBroken: boolean;      // Whether target note exists
  __blockId?: string;       // Optional block reference (future)
}
```

**Features:**
- Extends Lexical's `TextNode` for text editing capability
- Tracks broken state (visual feedback for missing notes)
- Supports block references: `[[Note Title#block-id]]`
- Full JSON serialization/import support
- ARIA labels for accessibility

**Broken Link Determination:**
```typescript
export function isLinkBroken(
  linkTitle: string,
  allNotes: Record<string, Note>
): boolean {
  // Link is broken if no note matches (exact or prefix)
  return !notesArray.some(
    (note) =>
      note.title.toLowerCase() === titleLower ||
      note.title.toLowerCase().startsWith(titleLower)
  );
}
```

#### B. **WikiLinkTransformPlugin - Pattern Detection**

**File:** `/src/components/editor/WikiLinkTransformPlugin.tsx` (215 lines)

**What It Does:**
1. **Real-time transformation:** As user types, scans text for `[[...]]` patterns
2. **Converts to WikiLinkNode:** Replaces raw text with WikiLinkNode instances
3. **Broken state detection:** Checks if linked note exists
4. **Click handling:** 
   - Valid links → navigate to target note
   - Broken links → show dialog to create new note
5. **State sync:** Updates link states when notes are created/deleted

**Event Handling:**
```typescript
// On text node transform
registerNodeTransform(TextNode, (textNode) => {
  // Find all [[...]] patterns
  const matches = text.match(/\[\[([^\]]+)\]\]/g);
  // Replace with WikiLinkNode instances
  // Update broken state based on note existence
});

// On click
registerCommand(CLICK_COMMAND, (event) => {
  if (target.classList.contains('wiki-link')) {
    if (isBroken) {
      // Show create dialog
    } else {
      // Navigate to target note
    }
  }
});
```

#### C. **WikiLinkAutocompletePlugin - Suggestions**

**File:** `/src/components/editor/WikiLinkAutocompletePlugin.tsx` (165 lines)

**Features:**
- Detects `[[` typing in editor
- Shows dropdown with matching notes
- Filters by current text: `[[que` → matches notes starting with "que"
- Displays note folder path for context
- Inserts complete `[[Note Title]]` when selected
- Smart dropdown positioning (below cursor or above if space needed)

**Pattern Detection:**
```typescript
const textBeforeCursor = text.slice(0, offset);
const match = textBeforeCursor.match(/\[\[([^\]]*?)$/);
// If match exists, show autocomplete
```

---

### 4. **BacklinksPanel Component (UI)**

**File:** `/src/components/BacklinksPanel.tsx` (220 lines)

**Displays Three Sections:**

#### **Section 1: Linked References** (Explicit Wiki-Links)
```
┌─ Linked References [3]
├─ 📄 Source Note 1 →
├─ 📄 Source Note 2 →
└─ 📄 Source Note 3 →
```
- Shows all notes that explicitly link to current note via `[[...]]`
- Click to navigate to source note
- Badge with count

#### **Section 2: Unlinked Mentions** (P1 Feature)
```
┌─ Unlinked Mentions [2] ▼
├─ 📄 Other Note A → [Link] button
│   "...text about [[Current Note]] context..."
└─ 📄 Other Note B → [Link] button
│   "...another mention of this note..."
```

**What It Does:**
- Finds text mentions of current note title (word-boundary matched)
- Shows context snippet (50 chars before/after)
- [Link] button converts mention to wiki-link with single click
- Filters: Must be 3+ chars, word-boundary match, not already linked

**Implementation:** `/src/utils/backlinks.ts:findUnlinkedMentions()`
```typescript
// Word-boundary regex to avoid partial matches
const titlePattern = new RegExp(`\\b${escapedTitle}\\b`, 'gi');

// Find matches not already surrounded by [[]]
while ((match = titlePattern.exec(content)) !== null) {
  if (beforeMatch !== '[[' || afterMatch !== ']]') {
    mentions.push({
      noteId, noteTitle, context, position
    });
  }
}
```

#### **Section 3: Broken Links** (P1 Feature)
```
┌─ Broken Links [1] ▼
└─ ⚠️ [[Non-existent Note]] → [Create] button
   "...context showing the broken link..."
```

- Shows all `[[...]]` links to notes that don't exist
- Context snippet shows where link appears
- [Create] button spawns new note with link title

**Implementation:** `/src/utils/backlinks.ts:findBrokenLinks()`

---

### 5. **Graph Data Processor (For Visualization)**

**File:** `/src/utils/graphDataProcessor.ts` (350+ lines)

**Purpose:** Transforms notes and their links into graph visualization data

**Graph Structures:**

**GraphNode:**
```typescript
interface GraphNode {
  id: string;           // Note ID or "tag:tagname"
  type: 'note' | 'tag';
  label: string;        // Display name
  color: string;        // Semantic token
  size: number;         // Based on connection count
  metadata: {
    createdAt?: string;
    folder?: string;
    tags?: string[];
  };
}
```

**GraphEdge:**
```typescript
interface GraphEdge {
  source: string;       // From note ID
  target: string;       // To note ID
  type: 'backlink' | 'tag';
  strength: number;     // For force simulation
}
```

**Build Process:**
1. Creates node for each note
2. Creates node for each tag
3. Adds edges for: backlinks (from `linkedNotes`) and tags
4. Applies filters (orphan hiding, focus mode, search)
5. Returns D3-ready data

---

### 6. **Store Integration (Zustand)**

**File:** `/src/stores/useNotesStore.ts`

**Backlink Actions:**
```typescript
interface NotesStore {
  // Get all notes that link TO this note
  getBacklinks: (noteId: string) => Note[];

  // Update the linkedNotes array based on content
  updateLinkedNotes: (noteId: string, content: string) => void;

  // P1: Convert unlinked mention to wiki-link
  convertToWikiLink: (noteId: string, position: number, targetTitle: string) => void;
}
```

**Implementation:**
```typescript
getBacklinks: (noteId: string) => {
  const note = state.notes[noteId];
  return getBacklinksUtil(noteId, note.title, state.notes);
}
```

**Note Type Definition:** `/src/types/notes.ts:60`
```typescript
export interface Note {
  // ...other fields...
  
  /** Notes that this note links TO (via [[Note Title]] syntax) */
  linkedNotes?: string[];
}
```

---

### 7. **Graph View Page**

**File:** `/src/pages/GraphView.tsx` (421 lines)

**Features:**
- **Force-directed D3 simulation** with draggable nodes
- **Focus mode:** Click node to zoom into N-hop neighbors
- **Color grouping:** By folder or tag
- **Node sizing:** By connection count
- **Link strength visualization:** Edge thickness = number of links
- **Search:** Find notes by name, tag, folder
- **Orphan detection & panel:** Find isolated notes with suggestions
- **Interactive legend:** Note types, connection types, statistics

**Key Controls:**
```
- Click node → Focus (show N-hop neighbors)
- Double-click → Open note
- Drag → Reposition
- Scroll → Zoom
- Search → Filter by name/tag
- "Color by" → Group by folder/tag
- "Size by connections" → Node size = # links
- "Orphans" → Show unlinked notes
```

---

## Data Flow: How Everything Connects

### **Creating a Wiki-Link:**

```
User types "[[Note Title]]"
    ↓
WikiLinkAutocompletePlugin detects [[ pattern
    ↓
Shows dropdown of matching notes
    ↓
User selects note
    ↓
AutocompletePlugin inserts "[[Note Title]]"
    ↓
WikiLinkTransformPlugin detects pattern
    ↓
Creates WikiLinkNode with isBroken=false
    ↓
Note saved
    ↓
updateLinkedNotes() extracts links from content
    ↓
Note.linkedNotes array updated in store
    ↓
GraphView updates with new edge
```

### **Viewing Backlinks:**

```
User opens a note
    ↓
BacklinksPanel renders
    ↓
getBacklinks(noteId) called
    ↓
Scans all notes for linkedNotes[].includes(noteId)
    ↓
Returns array of source notes
    ↓
BacklinksPanel displays with icons + navigation
```

### **Finding Unlinked Mentions:**

```
BacklinksPanel mounts
    ↓
findUnlinkedMentions(targetTitle, allNotes)
    ↓
For each note: scan content for word-boundary matches
    ↓
Filter: exclude if already [[bracketed]]
    ↓
Extract context (50 chars around match)
    ↓
Return [{ noteId, noteTitle, context, position }]
    ↓
Display with [Link] button
    ↓
User clicks [Link]
    ↓
convertToWikiLink(noteId, position, targetTitle)
    ↓
Replaces text mention with [[Target Title]]
```

## Key Technical Insights for Building a Backlinks Panel

### **1. Storage Strategy**

- **Primary:** `Note.linkedNotes` array stores IDs of notes THIS note links to
- **Query direction:** To find backlinks TO a note, scan all notes' `linkedNotes` arrays
- **Fallback:** Also parse content for `[[...]]` patterns (for edge cases)
- **Persistence:** Stored in IndexedDB via Zustand persist

### **2. Broken Link Detection**

- Happens at render time via `isLinkBroken(linkTitle, allNotes)`
- Matches: exact title (case-insensitive) or prefix match
- Used by WikiLinkNode for visual styling (red vs. blue)
- Used by WikiLinkTransformPlugin to show "Create note" dialog on click

### **3. Unlinked Mentions Algorithm**

**Requirements:**
- Title must be 3+ chars (reduce false positives)
- Word-boundary matching: `\b${escapedTitle}\b` (not partial matches)
- Check not already `[[bracketed]]`
- Limit to 20 results (performance)

**Output:** Array of `{ noteId, noteTitle, context, position }`

### **4. Context Extraction Pattern**

All three backlinks sections extract context:
```typescript
const contextStart = Math.max(0, position - 50);
const contextEnd = Math.min(content.length, position + matchLength + 50);
let context = content.substring(contextStart, contextEnd);
if (contextStart > 0) context = '...' + context;
if (contextEnd < content.length) context = context + '...';
```

### **5. Accessibility & UX**

**BacklinksPanel:**
- Icon + label for each section
- Badges show count
- Collapse/expand unlinked mentions & broken links
- Hover actions (→ chevron, [Link]/[Create] buttons)
- Click to navigate to source note

**WikiLinkNode:**
- `aria-label`: "Link to X" or "Broken link to X. Click to create note."
- Cursor pointer on hover
- Visual distinction (CSS classes: `wiki-link-valid` vs `wiki-link-broken`)

## Integration Points for Extensions

If building additional features on top of this:

1. **For new backlink sources:** Add to `getBacklinks()` query logic
2. **For graph visualization:** Modify `buildGraphData()` in graphDataProcessor
3. **For editor features:** Extend WikiLinkTransformPlugin or WikiLinkAutocompletePlugin
4. **For UI features:** BacklinksPanel is self-contained and can be enhanced independently
5. **For performance:** Both `getBacklinks()` and `findUnlinkedMentions()` limit results (20 mentions)

---

## Notable Patterns & Design Decisions

### **Pattern 1: Bi-directional Lookup**
- Notes store what they link TO (`linkedNotes` array)
- Backlinks computed on demand by scanning all notes
- Trade-off: Simpler writes, more reads (acceptable for local-first app)

### **Pattern 2: Dual Indexing**
- Primary: `linkedNotes` array (fast, explicit)
- Secondary: Parse content for `[[...]]` (fallback, complete)
- Ensures consistency even if array gets out of sync

### **Pattern 3: Lexical Custom Nodes**
- WikiLinkNode extends TextNode (not a separate node type)
- Preserves text editing capabilities
- Serializes full WikiLink metadata to/from JSON

### **Pattern 4: Real-time Transformation**
- WikiLinkTransformPlugin runs on every text change
- Regex-based detection (simple, no parsing overhead)
- Updates broken state when notes are created/deleted

### **Pattern 5: Context-Aware Autocomplete**
- Detects `[[` pattern with regex
- Positions dropdown intelligently (above/below cursor)
- Shows folder path for disambiguation
- Inserts full `[[Title]]` on selection
